bonuses.leadin: The method of “Universal Flowering,” involving the use of very small brushes and extremely sharp pencils, was used to create pieces in a style described by this adjective. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The method of "Universal Flowering," involving the use of very small brushes and extremely sharp pencils, was used to create pieces in a style described by this adjective. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this adjective which describes a style of art practiced by Pavel Filonov. Nearly monochromatic paintings like the Paris-inspired Ma Jolie were created by a different artist in a style described by this adjective.
While he bristled at the classification, Filonov is often viewed as a member of the Russian scene of this artistic movement, which celebrated the speed and machinery of the modern era.

This other artist’s influence on Russian Futurism is exemplified by a painting showing a cyclist in a brown jacket going past fragmented shop windows. This artist co-founded Rayonism with Mikhail Larionov.
parts_sanitized: Give this adjective which describes a style of art practiced by Pavel Filonov. Nearly monochromatic paintings like the Paris-inspired Ma Jolie were created by a different artist in a style described by this adjective.
While he bristled at the classification, Filonov is often viewed as a member of the Russian scene of this artistic movement, which celebrated the speed and machinery of the modern era.

This other artist's influence on Russian Futurism is exemplified by a painting showing a cyclist in a brown jacket going past fragmented shop windows. This artist co-founded Rayonism with Mikhail Larionov.
answers: analytical [or analytic; accept analytic cubism or analytical cubism; prompt on cubism or cubist by asking “Described by what other adjective?”] (Ma Jolie is by Pablo Picasso.)
answers_sanitized: analytical [or analytic; accept analytic cubism or analytical cubism; prompt on cubism or cubist by asking "Described by what other adjective?"] (Ma Jolie is by Pablo Picasso.)

bonuses.leadin: Name these Cubist paintings by Picasso, for 10 points each.
parts: A sailor, but not a plate of fruit, was removed between the studies and the final product of this 1907 painting, which drew on caricatures of African masks for the faces of five Barcelona prostitutes.
Picasso portrayed himself, Guillaume Apollinaire, and Max Jacob as a Harlequin, Pierrot, and monk in two later paintings of this title from his synthetic Cubist period.

This 1911 or 1912 painting contains guitar strings strummed by Picasso's lover Marcelle Humbert and a musical staff, among other nods to the titular refrain to a popular Parisian dance hall tune about "my pretty girl."
answers: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon [or The Young Ladies of Avignon]
answers_sanitized: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon [or The Young Ladies of Avignon]

This 1911 or 1912 painting contains guitar strings strummed by Picasso's lover Marcelle Humbert and a musical staff, among other nods to the titular refrain to a popular Parisian dance hall tune about "my pretty girl."
